channel.title: The Grant Desk - next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: next 30 days.

item.title: AY-ROOP / Le Milieu: Reuse - (Re)making for Circus Residency 2026 (Rennes Métropole, France)
item.description:
Organisation: AY-ROOP

Location: Le Milieu, Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole, France. Applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe country other than France and the French overseas territories (DROM-COM).

Award: Funded through Culture Moves Europe: EUR 900 mobility allowance per person plus flat-rate travel coverage; supplements for green mobility, visa fees, dependent children under 18, OCT/OR residents, and disability/accessibility needs (including possible full coverage of a sign-language interpreter). On-site single-room accommodation, lunches at Le Milieu canteen (Tuesday to Friday), shared kitchen, and technical support provided. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 28 November to 18 December 2026.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

AY-ROOP is launching an open call to select three circus artists and/or circus set designers/builders for a 21-day Culture Moves Europe research residency at Le Milieu (Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole) from 28 November to 18 December 2026. The residency is a research laboratory on reuse in contemporary circus: how everyday, locally available materials (paper/cardboard, plastics, lightweight metal, textiles, reclaimed wood, bicycle parts, etc.) can be used to create scenographic elements, sets, props and small apparatuses for juggling, acrobatics and balancing. The core principle is that objects are recreated locally from resources available in each territory rather than transported. AY-ROOP provides an equipped construction workshop, a rehearsal stage and circus tent, a guide to local reuse-sector contacts, and a full residency environment (accommodation, shared kitchen, working facilities); two mentors (a reuse specialist and a performing-arts professional) support the artists. PROFILES: circus artists interested in construction/scenography/DIY making, and builders/set designers working in circus; individual or group applications (max 3 people per group); emerging or established, any background. Accessible to artists with disabilities and accessibility needs. Working language English. No aerial apparatus or rigging: all work is ground-based. ELIGIBILITY: aged 18+ (no upper limit); legally resident in a Creative Europe country other than France and DROM-COM; available for the full residency. APPLY with a single PDF (1-page bio/CV, 5-page portfolio or video links, 1-page statement of intent, proof of residence). DEADLINE: 3 July 2026. Results announced 13 July 2026. Check the linked call PDF for full details.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEW INC Year 13 Mentor Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: NEW INC (New Museum Incubator)

Location: New York, NY, USA; primarily in-person at 250 Hudson St, lower Manhattan. Some hybrid participation possible.

Award: Dedicated Mentors: $500 honorarium. Office Hours Mentors: $300 honorarium. All mentors also receive a one-year All Access Plus membership to the New Museum. No application fee.

Duration: Year 13 program (September 2026 onward); average 4-8 hours of engagement per week.

Deadline: 08 Jul 2026

NEW INC, the New Museum's cultural incubator, is seeking mentors for Year 13 of its program supporting creative practitioners at the intersection of art, design, and technology. This is a call for mentors (creative and business leaders), not for program members. TWO MENTOR TYPES: Dedicated Mentors ($500 honorarium) are matched one-on-one with a member and commit to ongoing support throughout the year; Office Hours Mentors ($300 honorarium) offer one-on-one sessions to the broader community on an as-needed basis. All mentors receive a one-year All Access Plus membership to the New Museum, invitations to special events including the annual DEMO Festival, and access to community-only workshops, talks, and social events. YEAR 13 PROGRAM TRACKS mentors may support: Art & Code; Creative Science; Extended Realities; Launchpad; Social Architecture. KEY DATES: Mentor Open Call closes 8 July 2026; Mentor Open House (virtual info session) 17 June 2026, 12-1pm ET (register via the site); mandatory Dedicated Mentor matchmaking ranking session 11 September 2026; mandatory Dedicated Mentor matchmaking event 18 September 2026; Dedicated Mentor matches announced 2 October 2026. Most NEW INC activities take place Monday-Friday, 10am-6pm ET. Apply at https://www.newmuseum.org/new-inc/open-call/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Center for Humane Technology: Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship (Inaugural Cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Humane Technology (CHT)

Location: Remote, 10 hours/week overlapping with EST required.

Award: $30,000 fixed stipend paid in three milestone-based tranches. No application fee.

Duration: 6 months: September 14, 2026 through March 18, 2027. 15 hours per week.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

CHT's inaugural Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship places 3 fellows (one per focus area) in a 6-month, part-time program to develop rigorous public-impact research and writing on how AI is reshaping society. Fellows choose from 5 focus areas: (1) AI and Cognition — what AI does to human judgment, critical thinking, and intellectual independence; (2) AI and Relationships — AI's effects on intimacy, companionship, and social bonds; (3) AI and Surveillance — how AI enables monitoring, tracking, and control; (4) AI and Spirituality — AI's intersection with meaning, transcendence, and religious practice; (5) Inside the Machine: AI From a Technologist's Perspective. Up to 2 focus areas per application. DELIVERABLES: one flagship research piece, report, or long-form essay for public impact; 1-3 op-eds (placed in target outlets or CHT Substack); one podcast appearance (Your Undivided Attention or equivalent); cohort meetings and bi-weekly check-ins. SUPPORT: bi-weekly guidance from Senior Director of Strategy and Impact; editorial, communications and graphic design support; media training and PR placement support; CHT email address and Slack access; listing on CHT's public fellows page. WHO SHOULD APPLY: mid-career researchers (psychology, sociology, philosophy, cognitive science, communications, STS), policy analysts, journalists covering tech and society, civil society advocates, or technologists with insider perspective. Strong preference for those with a public writing track record (op-eds, Substack, peer-reviewed work) and some public presence. HOW TO APPLY: 1-2 page research proposal per topic (up to 2), CV/resume (2 pages max), 2-minute video explaining fit, writing sample. Deadline: Sunday 12 July 2026, 11:59pm EST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: European Commission: Safeguarding Europe's Born-Digital Heritage (PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL)
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission (Pilot Projects and Preparatory Actions, Culture strand)

Location: European Union. Consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU member states required.

Award: Up to €1,985,000, covering up to 85% of eligible project costs (15% co-financing required). No application fee.

Duration: Project duration to be defined in the proposal; covers a scoping and mapping phase across EU member states.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

The European Commission is offering a single grant of up to €1,985,000 (85% co-financing) for a project focused on safeguarding born-digital cultural heritage across EU member states. Scope includes: assessing the preservation state of born-digital art, web archives, video games, and digital content created outside cultural institutions; mapping EU and national legal frameworks; identifying non-legislative barriers (technical, financial, organisational, cultural); and providing guidance on creating preservation copies of licensed or legally accessible materials. This is a scoping and advocacy project, not a digitisation grant. ELIGIBILITY: the application must be led by a consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU countries, which rules out solo applicants and single-country organisations. Grant reference: PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL. Deadline: 16 July 2026. Verify the current deadline at ec.europa.eu before applying (one secondary source listed 1 June 2026; the primary call-fiche PDF and the Cyprus government funding portal both confirm 16 July 2026).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Longview Philanthropy: Digital Minds Research Fellowships 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Longview Philanthropy

Location: Worldwide / remote or institutional (grant-eligible countries listed in application form)

Award: $55,000–$120,000/year through a university, or $80,000–$150,000/year direct stipend; plus research budget (10% of salary), conference travel, and compute resources where applicable. No application fee.

Duration: 1–2 years. Second year conditional on continuing to meet programme requirements.

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

Longview Philanthropy Research Fellowships support scholars pursuing research on the potential consciousness, sentience, moral status, and legal position of AI systems. AMOUNT: $55,000–$120,000/year (through a university, with 10% research budget and standard fringe benefits), or $80,000–$150,000/year (direct stipend, with suggested 5–10% allocation for research and travel). Compute resources available for machine learning research. Invitation and funding to attend the annual Center for Mind, Ethics, and Policy Summit at NYU. ELIGIBILITY: must have or be expected to receive a doctoral degree in computer science, neuroscience, a related technical discipline, law, medicine, or applied social science by September 2027 or fellowship start date (exceptions for equivalent research experience); fellows arrange their own institutional affiliation; fellowship may be conditional on institutional affiliation or attendance at events. SELECTION CRITERIA: demonstrated interest in digital minds and its relation to AI risk; evidence of research promise (publications, academic achievements, references). DURATION: 1–2 years; second year conditional on ongoing programme requirements. AREAS OF INTEREST: introspection, welfare measurement, interpretability, model character, correlates of consciousness, valence research, governance frameworks, legal and political responses to potentially sentient AI. Deadline: 10 July 2026. Required application materials: CV, description of possible research directions (~1,000 words), intended outcomes, what you would do without this fellowship (~150–400 words), career goals (~300 words). Questions: digitalminds-rfp@longview.org. Apply at https://www.longview.org/request-for-proposals-research-and-applied-work-on-digital-minds/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Unruly Mirrors Open Call 2026 (East and Central Asia, Feminist Art)
item.description:
Organisation: Unruly Mirrors

Location: East and Central Asia (Kazakhstan, Japan, South Korea, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Uzbekistan, Mongolia)

Award: Financial support details not publicly confirmed at time of entry. No application fee confirmed.

Duration: Not publicly confirmed.

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Unruly Mirrors is an open call for artists based in Kazakhstan, Japan, South Korea, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Uzbekistan, or Mongolia. The project examines beauty practices in East and Central Asia as spaces of norm reproduction, intimacy, and feminist resistance. Financial support and full eligibility criteria were not publicly confirmed at time of entry (the website was unreachable). Deadline: 15 July 2026. Apply or inquire at https://unrulymirrors.com. NOTE: verify financial support and full details directly before applying.

Open call details

item.title: Longview Philanthropy: Digital Minds Grants for Applied Work 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Longview Philanthropy

Location: Worldwide / remote (grant-eligible countries listed in application form)

Award: Typical grants $50,000–$2,000,000 per project; proposals outside this range considered when the work justifies it. May include some operational support for new organisations. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; duration varies by scope.

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

Longview Philanthropy invites proposals for applied work on the potential consciousness, sentience, moral status, or experiences of AI systems. Typical grants are $50,000–$2,000,000 per project, with proposals outside that range considered when justified. New organisations may also receive some operational support. ELIGIBLE ACTIVITIES: seed funding for new organisations (think tanks, research bodies, initiatives); mentorship programmes for researchers entering the digital minds field; internships, new hires, or focused programmes on digital minds at existing AI organisations, academic labs, or policy institutions; convenings and field-building (workshops, conferences, summits, working groups addressing technical and governance questions). AREAS OF INTEREST: measuring AI welfare; consciousness and sentience research; governance and legal/social science frameworks for potentially sentient AI; communications work for thoughtful public discourse; research beyond the US; support organisations and operations professionals for independent technical researchers. SELECTION CRITERIA: potential impact, track record, demonstrated interest in digital minds and its relationship to AI risk, cost-effectiveness, legal compliance. Deadline: 10 July 2026. Questions: digitalminds-rfp@longview.org. Apply at https://www.longview.org/request-for-proposals-research-and-applied-work-on-digital-minds/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Revitalizing Rituals 2026 Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: Homo Faber Alapítvány (Homo Faber Foundation)

Location: Bhar Lazreg, Tunisia and Somogy County, Hungary; multi-stage in-person residency across both sites.

Award: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Multi-stage residency: Placemaking Camp (1 week, November 2026, Hungary); Storytellers Camp (1 week, March 2027, Tunisia); Residency Period (minimum 1 month, April-July 2027); public presentation (August 2027); Closing Event (3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary).

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Revitalizing Rituals invites artists from all disciplines -- sound, movement, visual arts, installation, performance, and transdisciplinary practices -- to join a research-creation journey across two landscapes: Bhar Lazreg in Tunisia and the wetlands of Somogy County in Hungary. The project explores rituals as living forms of belonging: reconnecting communities, land, memory, food, ecological reciprocity, and the more-than-human world. Selected artists work with local communities as co-participants in a shared inquiry into moving from extraction toward reciprocity. Organised by Homo Faber Alapítvány (Hungary). AMOUNT: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. ELIGIBILITY: minimum 5 years of active artistic practice; portfolio reflecting interest in situated or relational work; no formal degree required; working language is English (local language navigation valued); must be available for the full residency cycle including all international travel; must be able to legally receive the fee and invoice the organising partners from country of residence. Strongly encourages applications from historically marginalised or underrepresented voices. KEY DATES: Application Deadline 19 July 2026 (midnight) -- note the open call PDF also references a 5 July date; the officially promoted deadline is 19 July; Online Q&A 29 June 2026, 14:00 Tunisia / 15:00 CET (register at https://forms.gle/brnZvXvFe4JB8zL26); Online Conversations with shortlisted artists: mid July to mid August 2026; Selection Results: 31 August 2026; Placemaking Camp: 1 week, end of November 2026, Hungary; Storytellers Camp: 1 week, March 2027, Tunisia; Residency Period: minimum 1 month (splittable), April to July 2027; Final Presentation: August 2027; Closing Event: 3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary. Apply via the open call form linked at https://homofaber.hu/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Artist-Open-Call-FINAL-in-the-RR-form.pdf.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TBA21-Academy: Tentacular Fellowship 2026-2027 (Ocean Space, Venice)
item.description:
Organisation: TBA21-Academy, with NICHE Centre for Environmental Humanities and the UNESCO Chair on Water, Heritage and Sustainable Development at Ca' Foscari University

Location: Ocean Space and Ca' Foscari University, Venice, Italy; situated work in and with the communities of the Lagoon of Venice.

Award: Total fellowship support EUR 26,000. Phase 1 (incubation): EUR 3,000 grant for independent research and initiative development. Phase 2 (activation): EUR 8,000 grant plus a EUR 15,000 activation budget. No application fee.

Duration: 15 months, September 2026 to November 2027

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

TBA21-Academy, in collaboration with NICHE Centre for Environmental Humanities and the UNESCO Chair on Water, Heritage and Sustainable Development at Ca' Foscari University, is launching the first edition of the Tentacular Fellowship, an initiative-building fellowship based at Ocean Space and at Ca' Foscari in Venice. Conceived as a long-term platform for artistic research and ecological engagement, Ocean Space works to develop new forms of action capable of moving beyond awareness-raising to produce real, tangible territorial effects through situated collaboration with the communities, practitioners, researchers and civic actors already working within the Lagoon of Venice. The fellowship invites a practitioner or collective to initiate, consolidate or expand a situated initiative in the Lagoon through the creation of a Tentacular Unit: a collaborative, interdisciplinary platform able to gather artists, students, researchers, institutions, local communities and other actors around a specific socio-environmental challenge. Through situated research, alliance-building and public-facing formats, the fellow helps build the social, institutional and ecological conditions for the initiative to take root and grow over time. STRUCTURE: the two-year fellowship runs in two phases. In Phase 1 (incubation) the fellow is awarded a EUR 3,000 grant to support independent research and initiative development. In Phase 2 (activation) the fellow is awarded a EUR 8,000 grant plus a EUR 15,000 activation budget, for total fellowship support of EUR 26,000. Details on the application process, evaluation criteria and frequently asked questions are in the full open-call document. Application deadline: 5 July 2026, 11:59pm CET. Apply via the online form linked from the open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pioneer Works: 2027 Open Call – Visual Arts and Music Residency (Brooklyn, NY)
item.description:
Organisation: Pioneer Works

Location: Pioneer Works, Red Hook, Brooklyn, New York, USA

Award: Visual Arts: USD 5,000 one-time award (USD 2,500 honorarium + USD 500/month stipend for 5 months). Music: USD 3,000 one-time award (USD 2,500 honorarium + USD 500/month stipend for 1 month). Free studio space and facilities access. No housing or travel provided. No application fee.

Duration: Visual Arts: 5 months. Music: 1 month. Cohort begins after jury decisions in late September 2026.

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Pioneer Works in Red Hook, Brooklyn invites applications from emerging visual artists and musicians for its 2027 residency cohort. 12 Visual Arts residents (5-month residency) and 12 Music residents (1-month residency) are selected each year through a two-phase jury process. WHAT IS PROVIDED: free studio space; access to facilities including risograph printer, darkroom, ceramics studio, large-format printer, 3D printers, thermobinder, and laser cutter; biweekly studio visits with curators, gallerists, critics, and artists; free access to all public programmes at Pioneer Works, including Second Sundays (monthly open studios and performances). FINANCIAL AWARD: USD 5,000 for Visual Arts residents; USD 3,000 for Music residents (each comprising a USD 2,500 honorarium plus USD 500/month for the residency duration); applicants' choice on how to spend it. Pioneer Works does not provide housing, travel, or visa sponsorship. ELIGIBILITY: open locally, nationally, and internationally; collectives may apply (resources shared); current students and programme alumni are not eligible. REVIEW PROCESS: phase one is internal scoring on technical ability, uniqueness of vision, and future potential (nontraditional career paths given special consideration); highest scorers advance to an external jury of art professionals and alumni who assess diversity of demographics and media, genuine studio need, and fit with Pioneer Works' public-facing culture. Jury convenes September 2026; all applicants notified by late September. Supported by Dalio Philanthropies, the Andy Warhol Foundation, NYC Department of Cultural Affairs, New York State Council on the Arts, and the National Endowment for the Arts. APPLY: via Pioneer Works website by 13 July 2026. Questions: residency@pioneerworks.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 (Documentary Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Women Photograph (supported by Leica USA)

Location: Remote (international)

Award: Three grants of USD 10,000 each (USD 30,000 total). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (new or in-progress photography projects).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 awards three grants of USD 10,000 each to support documentary photography projects. ELIGIBILITY: open to women, nonbinary, transgender, and intersex documentary photographers of any nationality; no membership required; no restrictions on camera equipment or career stage. Funding covers hard costs of reporting, photographers' creative fees, and any other expenses that support the production of new or in-progress still photography projects. Not intended for books, exhibitions, or workshops. Grantees retain full work ownership. One submission per applicant; applications in English (Google Translate acceptable); any use of generative AI must be disclosed. Applications opened 20 May 2026; deadline 30 June 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Apply at https://app.picter.com/contests/bad7ea97-7b0d-4a9c-9e85-38e73529d62b/start.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Nakanojo Biennale 2027: International Artist Open Call (Gunma Prefecture, Japan)
item.description:
Organisation: Nakanojo Biennale Executive Committee / Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture

Location: Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture, Japan

Award: Production grant provided (amount not specified publicly). No exhibition fee, no residency fee. Insurance subsidies available. Artists responsible for materials, food, transportation, and installation/removal costs. No application fee.

Duration: Exhibition: 18 September – 24 October 2027 (37 days). Production period: April–August 2027. Residency accommodation available from April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Nakanojo Biennale 2027 (11th edition) is an international open call for artists of all genres — sculpture, painting, photography, video art, installation, performance, and more — to exhibit in Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. Theme: 'Echoing Green', inspired by William Blake's poem, exploring cycles of life, regeneration, and connection between past and future generations. Open internationally to individual artists and artist groups at all career stages; new participants are encouraged. A production grant is provided (amount not publicly specified); no exhibition or residency fees are charged. Residency accommodation available from April 2027. Staff support provided for venue selection and installation guidance. First-time participants in Japan must attend an on-site venue inspection; an online orientation on 5 September 2026 is required for all accepted artists. Deadline: 12 July 2026 at 23:59 JST (online). Apply via Google Form at https://forms.gle/SS8DaaGA6uwUKogj7. Full details at https://nakanojo-biennale.com/2027-open-call/english.html. Contact: office@nakanojo-biennale.com.

Open call details

item.title: Samstag International Visual Arts Scholarships - Class of 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Samstag Museum of Art, University of South Australia

Location: Australia (applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents); 12-month placement at an international institution of the scholar's choice.

Award: AUD $75,000 tax-exempt stipend; institutional fees for one academic year of study (where applicable); return airfares; and travel and health insurance. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months overseas at an international learning institution.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Samstag International Visual Arts Scholarships are annual scholarships enabling Australian artists to develop their artistic capacities through a dedicated year of practice-based learning at an international institution of their choice. AMOUNT: AUD $75,000 tax-exempt stipend; institutional fees for one academic year (where applicable); return airfares; and travel and health insurance. ELIGIBILITY: Australian citizens or permanent residents aged 18 or over who are either (a) currently enrolled in a practice-led visual arts program at an Australian institution of higher education, having successfully completed at least two years of an undergraduate or postgraduate degree; or (b) graduates of a practice-led visual arts undergraduate or postgraduate qualification at an Australian institution of higher education (graduates of more than five years' standing are now eligible following 2023 guideline updates). PROCESS: applicants must create an account in the online portal, download the Samstag Microsoft PowerPoint template, read the Class of 2027 Guidelines, and complete and submit the online application form. Accounts can be created before the deadline and applications saved for later submission. Prior applicants (Classes of 2024, 2025, or 2026) can reuse the same account. Deadline: 30 June 2026, midnight Australian Central Standard Time (ACST). Apply at https://samstag.adelaide.edu.au/scholarship/apply/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Small File Media Festival 2026 - Call for Work
item.description:
Organisation: Small File Media Society

Location: Canada; festival screening. Open to international submissions.

Award: No payment stated; festival programming and screening. No submission fee stated.

Duration: One-time festival screening.

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

The Small File Media Festival 2026 invites moving-image submissions under the theme "Can Small Files Save the World?" -- a call for arthouse, video work, glitch aesthetics, punk, sci-fi, narrative, and documentary work made with a small-file mindset. This year the festival especially welcomes small-scale experiments with narrative and documentary. The festival frames Small File Ecomedia as a political and ecological intervention: small files function in regions with precarious infrastructure, circumvent bandwidth imperialism and surveillance, and expose the environmental cost of mainstream online video. WHAT THEY WANT: craftily composed, elegantly performed, and artfully minimised movies in any genre; use of obsolete technologies, creative compression techniques, and low-bandwidth aesthetics at all stages of production is encouraged. Resources for making small-file work at www.smallfile.ca/make. Open to artists, activists, media pirates, rogue technologists, and filmmakers. No payment or submission fee stated. All submitted information used for festival programming and promotion -- ensure accurate spelling, punctuation, and capitalisation. Submissions close 1 July 2026 at midnight. Submit at https://smallfile.ca/submit/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Richell Prize for Emerging Writers 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Emerging Writers' Festival and Hachette Australia

Location: Australia; must currently reside in Australia.

Award: AUD $10,000 prize money (donated by Hachette Australia); 12-month mentorship with a Hachette Australia publisher; first option to consider finished work for publication. Long- and short-listed writers also eligible to apply for a Casa Morphosi Residency in Abruzzo, Italy. No application fee.

Duration: 12-month mentorship.

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

The Richell Prize for Emerging Writers, presented by the Emerging Writers' Festival and Hachette Australia, is an annual prize for unpublished writers of adult fiction and adult narrative non-fiction. The winner receives AUD $10,000 and a 12-month mentorship with a Hachette Australia publisher, who will work with the writer to develop their manuscript, with first option to consider the finished work for publication. Long- and short-listed writers are also eligible to apply for a Casa Morphosi Residency in Abruzzo, Italy. The prize was established in memory of Hachette Australia CEO Matt Richell (d. 2014). ELIGIBILITY: must currently reside in Australia (Australian citizens, permanent residents, and long-term visa holders residing in Australia; those residing overseas are not eligible); unpublished writers only -- no previous publication by a publishing house (including small press or independent publishers in any genre); publication in anthologies, literary journals, scholarly works, writing PhD projects, or self-publishing does not disqualify. ELIGIBLE GENRES: adult fiction (including short story collections) and adult narrative non-fiction (including memoir and essay collections). Poetry, scripts, plays, picture books, practical non-fiction, and works for children or young adults are not eligible. SUBMISSION FORMAT: single PDF comprising the first three chapters (maximum 20,000 words), a one-page synopsis, and a chapter breakdown of up to two A4 pages; plus a statement (up to 750 words) on how winning the prize will further your writing career, entered in the online form. Manuscript must be complete or intended to be completed; does not need to be finished at time of submission. A4, double-spaced, 12pt Roman font (e.g. Garamond or Times New Roman), 3cm margins, page numbers. TIMELINE: prize opened 23 April 2026; closes 13 July 2026 at 11:59pm AEST; longlist announced 11 September 2026; shortlist 16 October 2026; winner announced 6 November 2026. Administered with support from Simpsons Solicitors. Apply at https://emergingwritersfestival.org.au/richell-prize-2026/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026: Open Call (Vienna, Austria)
item.description:
Organisation: Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography

Location: Academy of Fine Arts, Vienna, Austria

Award: EUR 250 cash award per selected winner + EUR 150 travel allowance. Grand Prix winner receives an additional EUR 250, a solo exhibition, and a free hotel stay. Submission fee: EUR 20 per project.

Duration: Exhibition at Academy of Fine Arts Vienna; announcement August 2026, public reveal September 2026.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026, organised by Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography, selects 20 projects for exhibition at the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna. Theme: 'Anthropocene: Hybrid Realities' – exploring the tension between human ecological dominance and environmental fragility. Each selected winner receives: exhibition in a 1,300+ m² venue, publication in the official festival catalogue (2 copies), EUR 250 cash award, EUR 150 travel allowance, festival pass, and free registration for Photodays Rovinj 2027. Grand Prix winner additionally receives a solo exhibition, an extra EUR 250, a free hotel stay, and a catalogue cover feature. ELIGIBILITY: open to any individual aged 16 or older, at any career stage, internationally; submissions must include at least one analog photographic process; works must have been taken within the last 5 years (2021-2026); no AI-generated or AI-enhanced work (unless deliberately thematic). Files must be JPEG, max 5 MB, minimum 2000px on longest side, 300 dpi. Jury selects winners by late July 2026. Deadline: 30 June 2026. NOTE: EUR 20 submission fee per project applies. Apply at https://www.rotlicht-festival.at/open-call-submission/.

item.title: GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 - Research Track
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)

Location: London, UK; in-person, full-time.

Award: £12,000 salary for the three-month term, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch provided. Visa sponsorship (3-month temporary work visa) available. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 18 January to 9 April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

GovAI's UK Winter Fellowship (Research Track) is a three-month in-person fellowship in London for researchers and policy professionals seeking to enter or advance careers in AI governance and policy. Fellows conduct independent research on a topic of their choice, paired with a GovAI supervisor from the team or network; the first two weeks are spent exploring the AI governance landscape before a research proposal is finalised. Research outputs may include a report, white paper, journal article, op-ed, or blog post targeted at an audience relevant to AI governance. Alongside the project, fellows attend expert Q&As, weekly seminars, skills workshops, work-in-progress meetings, and social events, and are encouraged to discuss follow-on career opportunities with GovAI's team and network. GovAI alumni have gone on to roles in US, UK, and EU government; leading AI companies (Anthropic, DeepMind, OpenAI); top think tanks (CSET, RAND); and leading universities (Oxford, Cambridge). AMOUNT: £12,000 salary for three months, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch. Visa sponsorship for a 3-month temporary work visa is available; successful visa applicants must remain in their country of application while the visa is processed. ELIGIBILITY: open to a wide range of backgrounds (government, academia, industry, civil society); no strict degree requirements; relevant areas include public policy, political science, computer science, economics, biosecurity, cybersecurity, China studies, and risk management. Senior candidates who need accommodations should email fellowships@governance.ai with subject line 'senior candidate winter fellowship 2027' and a CV. SELECTION CRITERIA: quality and clarity of written work, relevant expertise, judgement in prioritising research directions, and team fit (intellectual honesty, openness to feedback, comfort with uncertainty). APPLICATION PROCESS: (1) written application and automated abstract reasoning assessment; (2) paid remote work test (August 2026); (3) remote interview and reference checks (September 2026); decisions communicated October 2026. Deadline: 12 July 2026, 23:59 BST.

Open call details

item.title: Claude Corps Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Anthropic (funded); CodePath (employer of record); Social Finance (evaluation)

Location: USA; in-person at host nonprofit organizations across America (placement location varies; relocation support available).

Award: $85,000 full-time annual salary plus benefits, expansive Claude token budget, and relocation support as needed. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months.

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

Claude Corps is a national fellowship program from Anthropic placing early-career fellows full-time at nonprofits across America for one year, with the goal of extending AI's benefits to communities and building fellows' AI skills. Anthropic funds the program and provides Claude expertise; CodePath (America's largest collegiate CS education provider) is the fellows' official employer of record and leads programming; Social Finance leads measurement and evaluation. AMOUNT: $85,000 full-time salary plus benefits; expansive Claude token budget; relocation support as needed. FELLOW EXPERIENCE: intensive Claude training at the start; five hours of ongoing weekly training throughout; support from a CodePath mentor; Anthropic office hours for technical questions; and professional guidance from the host organization manager. SAMPLE HOST ORGANIZATIONS: Braven (Chicago), Code the Dream (Durham NC), Heartland Forward (Bentonville AR), Montgomery County Food Bank (Conroe TX), Reef Environmental Education Foundation (Key Largo FL), StriveTogether (Cincinnati), YMCA of Greater Charlotte, and more than 400 nonprofits total. ELIGIBILITY: anyone over 18 with under two years of full-time work experience, regardless of educational background; must be authorized to work in the US; must be comfortable working with Claude and willing to relocate if necessary. COHORTS: Cohort 1 (100 fellows, starts October 2026) -- applications close 17 July 2026; Cohort 2 (starts January 2027) and Cohort 3 (starts August 2027) -- rolling applications. Apply at https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-corps.

Location: Aix-Marseille, France (campus residency)

Iméra's 'This Is Not Another Fellowship' is a 3-month postdoctoral residency at the Institute for Advanced Study at Aix Marseille Université, offered in two semester options: November 2, 2026 – January 26, 2027, or March 1, 2027 – May 28, 2027. PROVIDED: free accommodation in one of fourteen campus apartments; travel expenses covered; desk in shared open co-working space; participation in the weekly Community Building Seminar (mandatory 3-hour Thursday sessions); access to interdisciplinary scholarly networks. item.title: Rodger and Joann McLennan Canadian War Art Research Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: National Gallery of Canada

Location: Ottawa, Canada; tenable only at the National Gallery of Canada.

Award: Up to $5,000 (including expenses and/or stipend). No application fee.

Duration: One calendar year, beginning 1 September 2026.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

The National Gallery of Canada's Rodger and Joann McLennan Canadian War Art Research Fellowship encourages and supports advanced research in Canadian and Indigenous Canadian war and military art. Research must relate to any aspect of Canadian war or military art (including painting, drawing, sculpture, photography, printmaking, and other media) and must emphasise the use and investigation of the collections of the National Gallery of Canada, the Canadian War Museum, and Library and Archives Canada. The Fellowship supports original research by scholars undertaking publishing, exhibition, thesis, and dissertation work or their equivalents. AMOUNT: up to $5,000, including expenses and/or stipend. TENURE: one calendar year beginning 1 September 2026; tenable only at the National Gallery of Canada. ELIGIBILITY: open to art historians, curators, critics, conservators, graduate students, and independent or other professionals working in the visual arts, museology, and related disciplines in the humanities and social sciences; international applicants welcome. Researchers from outside Canada must ensure all visa requirements and documentation are in order before entry and the start of the Fellowship. Applications are invited from all qualified candidates, including Indigenous peoples, women, people of any sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression, racialized people, and people with disabilities. TO APPLY: submit a complete application in English or French as a single PDF by email to Amy Rose, Senior Manager, Library, Archives and Research Fellowship Program, at erefel@gallery.ca. Deadline: 26 June 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Expo Bart: OPEN HALL 2026 (Free Project Space, Technique & Support)
item.description:
Organisation: Expo Bart

Location: Expo Bart, NYMA, Waalplein 6, Nijmegen, Netherlands

Award: No fee and no rent, but also no artist fee or other compensation (geen vergoeding). Provides free use of a 100 m2 fully blackout-able project space, limited technique (2 short-throw beamers, a PA set with mixer and microphones, and light spots) and helping hands, plus promotion of your event via Expo Bart's channels.

Duration: Programme runs 13 November to 20 December 2026; book one or more days (open Thursday to Sunday, 12:00-17:00; other times negotiable)

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Do you have an idea you have always wanted to carry out but can never find the space for? Expo Bart in Nijmegen invites you to apply for OPEN HALL. From 13 November to 20 December 2026, Expo Bart makes its project space, technique and helping hands available to help realise your idea, whether that is a week-long exhibition, a one-day workshop, a film shoot, a knitting club, a concert series, a film night, a performance, a dinner, a combination of all of these, or something completely different. PRACTICAL: you can sign up for one or more days between 13 November and 20 December 2026; Expo Bart is open Thursday to Sunday from 12:00 to 17:00 (organising something outside opening hours can be discussed together); the 100 m2 project space is fully blackout-able; limited technique is available, namely two short-throw beamers, a PA set (with mixer and microphones) and light spots; and your event is of course shared via Expo Bart's channels. There is no compensation and no rent is charged. APPLY by sending your idea (max 1 A4, including photos) to info@expobart.nl with the subject line 'OPEN HALL', stating your plan/idea, your preferred day(s), and what you need in terms of production. Deadline: 12 July 2026.

item.title: Fluxus Experimental Film & Media Arts Festival 2026: Call for Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Hamilton Artists Inc., in partnership with McMaster University School of the Arts and Factory Media Centre

Location: Hamilton, Ontario, Canada; submitting artists must be currently based and/or working in Canada.

Award: Free to submit (NO entry fee). IMAA-scale artist remuneration (a screening/exhibition fee following the Independent Media Arts Alliance fee schedule) is paid to successful applicants within 30 days of the screenings.

Duration: Two screenings, 18 and 19 September 2026

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Fluxus is a contemporary short film and media arts festival hosted in partnership between Factory Media Centre, Hamilton Artists Inc and McMaster University School of the Arts. Its mission is to provide an accessible forum for the exhibition of creative, experimental, moving-image art forms, and to develop connections between Canadian media artists, arts institutions and the public. Fluxus showcases experimental and boundary-pushing media works from artists of all levels working in Canada; submissions are free and unrestricted by theme, open to a spectrum of artforms including but not limited to animation, documentary, video art, collage film, music video and experimental film. The festival takes place over two screenings at Hamilton Artists Inc on Friday 18 and Saturday 19 September 2026 (the Friday screening is dedicated to emerging media artists and filmmakers); all screenings are free to attend. REMUNERATION: there is no submission fee, and IMAA-scale artist remuneration is paid to successful applicants within 30 days of the screenings. GUIDELINES: artists must be currently based and/or working in Canada; work must be short format (15 minutes or less) and no more than 5 years old (2021 or later); submissions are digital Vimeo or YouTube links (private/password-protected accepted if the password is provided); solo artists, groups, collectives and collaborative works are welcome, with a limit of one submission per artist/group/collective; films must be independent projects over which the artist retains full creative and artistic control. AI POLICY: Fluxus discourages the use of generative AI in submitted works; if AI factors into the creative process, its use must be declared and described (see Factory Media Centre's Policy on the Use of Generative AI). APPLY via the online submission form with an artist bio (75 words max), artist CV (PDF, 3 pages max), a brief description of the work (100 words max), entry information, the video link, and two image stills. Notification is expected in August; due to the high quantity of submissions, only selected artists will be contacted. Fluxus is committed to equity and strongly encourages applications from Black and Indigenous artists, persons with disabilities, ethnic or racial minorities, immigrants and refugees, Francophones, LGBTQI+ persons, women, and persons of all socio-economic backgrounds. Deadline: Wednesday 15 July 2026 at 11:59pm EST. Questions: programming@theinc.ca.

Open call details

item.title: Frame Fellowship 2.0 (Residential Accelerator for AI Safety Content Creators) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Frame Fellowship

Location: San Francisco, USA; residential (in person).

Award: USD 10,000 stipend for the 10 weeks, plus accommodation, meals and flights covered. Access to production-ready studios in San Francisco, a mentor and creator network, and connections to funding, sponsorship and partnership opportunities at organizations such as FLI, CAIS and Seismic. Top performers may be eligible for additional grant opportunities beyond the program. No application fee.

Duration: 10-week residential program

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

Frame Fellowship is a 10-week residential accelerator in San Francisco for content creators educating the public on AI safety. Frame's premise is that content creators can solve a long-standing bottleneck of expanding reach and trust beyond the AI safety and effective altruism (EA) bubble; creators today hack attention, empower mass action and shape culture. Past Frame Fellows include award-winning filmmakers, journalists from leading newsrooms and ex-activists who transitioned to educating, empowering and preparing their communities for the impacts of AGI. Frame helps amplify their impact to millions of people, connects them to funding and sponsorship opportunities at organizations such as FLI, CAIS and Seismic, and helps establish their path as a leading content creator. This cohort runs 10 weeks with a stronger mentor and creator network, access to production-ready studios in San Francisco, housing, food and a stipend, plus connections to funding and partnership opportunities after the program. TWO TRACKS: (1) Independent Track, build your own channel and become a defining voice on a topic; (2) Amplifier Track, embed with an AI org/company and grow their reach. SUPPORT: Frame covers accommodation, meals, flights and a USD 10,000 stipend for the 10 weeks so fellows can go all in; top performers may be eligible for additional grant opportunities beyond the program. Early deadline: 10 July 2026. Apply at https://framefellowship.com/.

Open call details

item.title: FUTURES Photography: 2027 Residency Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: FUTURES Photography

Location: Futures Hub, Amsterdam, Netherlands; ISSP, Riga, Latvia; Capa Center, Budapest, Hungary

Award: EUR 1,000 stipend; accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 1 month; location-specific dates in 2027 (Amsterdam: Jan, Feb, Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec; Riga: May; Budapest: Apr)

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

ELIGIBILITY: Open exclusively to current FUTURES artists (existing members of the FUTURES Photography platform); the application is free of charge. CONCEPT: The residency supports emerging photographers with time, space, and resources to develop new work, with a strong emphasis on research and artistic process. Artists are invited to explore the intersection of Nature, Environment, and Technology - projects should challenge perceptions of human and ecological relationships, technological advancement, and environmental responsibility. An interdisciplinary approach is encouraged, and each residency culminates in a public event where artists share their work and engage with diverse audiences. LOCATIONS: Three partner venues host residencies at different times in 2027. (1) Futures Hub, Amsterdam (global FUTURES HQ): a 60 sqm ground-floor studio with heating, electricity, and private entrance; live-work space with double folding bed, private bathroom, washing machine, and small kitchen; sliding door separates work and living areas; access to a shared atrium for large-scale works, performances, and presentations; communal rooftop terrace; bed linens and towels provided; available January, February, April, May, June, September, October, November, December 2027. (2) ISSP, Riga: private bedroom with working space in a shared city-centre apartment with two other artists; spacious shared living room and fully equipped kitchen; access to ISSP office facilities including printer, scanner, ISSP photography library, and studio lights; available May 2027. (3) Capa Center, Budapest: fully furnished private room in a communal apartment at Art Quarter Budapest; 400 sqm shared studio in a postindustrial area; services to integrate residents into the local Budapest art scene and establish professional connections; option to organise a presentation at Capa Centre in downtown Budapest; access to Capa Centre office facilities (printer, scanner); available April 2027. PRESENCE: minimum 90% presence required throughout the residency period. ENGAGEMENT: residents contribute to a Visual Diary (one Instagram carousel and two Stories, in collaboration with FUTURES), a short Video Portrait documenting their process, a public event such as an open studio or workshop (open studios on the last Wednesday of the month), and a written interview with a local writer published on the FUTURES website. FINANCIAL SUPPORT: EUR 1,000 stipend to cover living expenses, materials, and travel; accommodation is provided at all locations. Residents are responsible for arranging travel documents, health and travel insurance, liability insurance, and any visa requirements (support provided where needed). APPLY: via the FUTURES website; application is free and open only to FUTURES artists. TIMELINE: deadline 22 June 2026 at 23:59 Amsterdam time; results announced July 2026. Contact info@futures-photography.com for questions.

Open call details

item.title: TechCongress: Congressional Innovation Fellowship 2027 (Senior and Early-Career)
item.description:
Organisation: TechCongress

Location: Washington, DC, United States. Full-time, in-person; remote participation is not possible. Nonpartisan: fellows serve in both Democratic and Republican offices.

Award: Senior (mid-career) Fellows: USD 100,600/year stipend. Early-career Fellows: USD 78,000 annual-equivalent stipend (USD 6,500/month). Plus health insurance supplement up to USD 425/month, up to USD 2,000 relocation to DC, up to USD 2,000 first-month short-term housing reimbursement, up to USD 2,000 official travel, and a USD 500 professional-attire allowance. No application fee.

Duration: Senior Fellows serve January to December 2027 (optional one-month extension). Early-career Fellows serve January to October 2027.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

TechCongress places technologists in the US Congress to build technology capacity among Members and Congressional staff, where fewer than 3% of legislative staff have a technical background. For the January 2027 cohort it is recruiting up to twenty fellows (10 for Democratic offices, 10 for Republican offices) across two tiers: the Senior Congressional Innovation Fellowship (mid-career, at least eight years of work or postgraduate study) and the Congressional Innovation Fellowship (early-career, two to six years of experience, including a recently finished or projected-by-January-2027 graduate technical degree such as a Masters or PhD; applicants with seven years qualify for an intermediate tier). Fellows choose a placement with a Member of Congress or a Congressional Committee, report to a senior staffer (such as a Chief of Staff or Staff Director), and do work similar to other Congressional staff: researching tech policy (cyber and election security, data and biometric privacy, AI policy, autonomous vehicles, health IT, encryption, disinformation), educating Members and staff, writing legislation, preparing Committee hearings, markups and investigations, and building coalitions. No prior government experience is required; the fellowship is first and foremost an educational experience. This year TechCongress also seeks at least two fellows from non-traditional verticals: climate (grid modernization, clean energy, NEPA reform), government innovation (govtech and digital benefits delivery, civil service reform), and science, research and innovation (NSF, NIH, DoE, FDA reform, clinical trials). Technical degrees or experience in engineering, development, journalism, scientific, legal or government roles are encouraged. TechCongress is a nonpartisan nonprofit incubated at the Open Technology Institute at New America; since 2016 it has sent 140 fellows to Congress. Equal opportunity employer. Application deadline: 26 June 2026, 5:59am (applicant's local timezone). Contact: aleena@techcongress.io. Apply via the official application form.

Open call details

item.title: APWLD: Breaking Out of Marginalisation (BOOM) Feminist Participatory Action Research, Feminist Food Sovereignty 2026-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development (APWLD)

Location: Asia and the Pacific; open to national and/or grassroots women-led organisations in the region (Central Asia, East Asia and the Pacific sub-regions especially encouraged).

Award: Sub-grant of up to USD 14,000 per selected partner organisation to support the employment of a young woman researcher and cover direct research, implementation, advocacy and capacity-building costs (not institutional sustainability or maintenance). Also includes capacity-building workshops, regional training, advocacy and networking opportunities, and ongoing accompaniment. No application fee.

Duration: 16-month FPAR implementation (2026-2028 cycle)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

APWLD invites grassroots women's organisations and movements in Asia and the Pacific to take part in a Feminist Participatory Action Research (FPAR) cycle on Feminist Food Sovereignty, supporting women-led and community-driven research, collective action and advocacy that reclaim community control over food systems, natural resources and knowledge while challenging corporate control and structural inequalities in food and agriculture. Six national grassroots women-led organisations will be selected to work with their communities to conduct FPAR, generate community-led evidence and develop collective advocacy from 2026 to 2028. THEMATIC STREAMS (partners may focus on one or more): community-led sustainable food systems (women-led agroecology, ecological farming, biodiversity restoration, farmer-to-farmer learning); seed sovereignty and Indigenous knowledge systems (women as seed keepers, community seed banks, resistance to corporate seed regimes); land, water and territorial rights (struggles against land and water grabbing, community governance of commons); and access to food justice and systemic transformation (legal, policy and advocacy strategies, cooperatives, community markets, alternative food economies). APWLD prioritises initiatives that strengthen Indigenous knowledge, collective governance, ecological sustainability, feminist transformation and women's leadership. SUPPORT: a sub-grant of up to USD 14,000 per partner over 16 months. Each selected partner identifies two women, a mentor and a young woman researcher below 35, at least one from the community or constituency where the organisation has ongoing food sovereignty work (grassroots, rural or Indigenous women, small-scale food producers, peasants, fisherfolk, or women advancing women-led food sovereignty alternatives). ELIGIBILITY: non-governmental, non-profit, women-led and/or grassroots organisations with experience working with grassroots women, ongoing food-sovereignty work, capacity to conduct participatory feminist community-led research, and ability to work in English (the main working language for training and reporting); a recommendation/reference letter from at least one women's or grassroots organisation is required. APPLY via the online Google Form or by emailing the completed application form to eloisa@apwld.org and tasmiah@apwld.org. Deadline: Sunday 5 July 2026. NOTE: this is an organisation-only sub-grant in the food sovereignty / agroecology and gender-justice space rather than an individual arts/AI opportunity.

item.title: Upstream Creative Writers / Grassroots Journalism Residency 2026: Writing About Natural Places, Stewards, and the Spaces We Care For
item.description:
Organisation: Upstream

Location: Remote residency in Minnesota, USA; applicants must be Minnesota residents living in East Side St Paul; West Central Minnesota (Becker, Clay, Douglas, Grant, Otter Tail, Pope, Stevens, Traverse, and Wilkin Counties); or the Iron Range (Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties).

Award: USD 3,000 artist stipend, plus mileage reimbursement up to USD 500, a Minnesota State Parks Pass, mentorship and creative support, and the opportunity to publish and share work statewide. No application fee.

Duration: 9-month remote residency

Deadline: 06 Jul 2026

The Upstream Creative Writers / Grassroots Journalism Residency invites creative writers, poets, storytellers and grassroots journalists to spend intentional time over nine months exploring Minnesota's parks, waterways, forests and natural places, and the people who care for them. The residency seeks writers who care about storytelling rooted in place, environment, community, stewardship and connection. SELECTED MINNESOTA ARTISTS RECEIVE: a USD 3,000 artist stipend; mileage reimbursement up to USD 500; a Minnesota State Parks Pass; mentorship and creative support; and the opportunity to publish and share their work statewide. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be a Minnesota resident, 18 years or older, with a residence in East Side St Paul; West Central Minnesota (Becker, Clay, Douglas, Grant, Otter Tail, Pope, Stevens, Traverse, and Wilkin Counties); or the Iron Range, Minnesota (Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties). Applications open 8 June 2026; deadline 6 July 2026 by 11:59pm. Apply via the online JotForm.

item.title: Sandberg Instituut: Call for Proposals, Temporary Department 2027-2029 (Head of Department, The Archive)
item.description:
Organisation: Sandberg Instituut

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; applicants must be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate for the duration of the programme.

Award: Freelance position as Head of the Temporary Department, approx. two days per week over two academic years (September 2027 to July 2029), plus allocated hours for curriculum development (October 2026 to July 2027). Approximate daily rate EUR 553.60. The department is also supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a dedicated department coordinator plus administrative support, and institutional infrastructure and guidance. A modest fee is provided for the second-round interview stage. No application fee.

Duration: Two-year Temporary Department, September 2027 to July 2029 (curriculum development from October 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

Sandberg Instituut invites artists, designers, researchers, makers and educators to propose a Temporary Department, taking on the role of Head of Department for the period September 2027 to July 2029. THEME, THE ARCHIVE: for this edition the institute seeks proposals that engage with the archive as a contemporary artistic, social, political and pedagogical framework. Increasingly recognised as a site of power, selection, memory, erasure and imagination, the archive is approached here not as a static collection but as a living, contested and generative process to be activated, reinterpreted and transformed through contemporary practice, and as a material and methodological field to work with, against and through. Possible starting points include: archives as sites of resistance, repair and restitution; embodied, oral and performative archives; collective and community-based archives; digital, algorithmic and AI-driven processes; counter-archives and practices of refusal; speculative archives and future-oriented forms of memory; creative practices that activate, reinterpret or transform existing archives; and the politics of preservation, access and knowledge production. Proposals should articulate a compelling approach to the archive plus a clear, connected pedagogical vision for how collective research unfolds over two years while supporting individual trajectories; partnerships with external organisations (museums, social archives, artist-led initiatives, community organisations and research institutions) are encouraged. FORMAT: Temporary Departments are full-time two-year master's studies bringing a cohort of students and tutors together around a specific field of inquiry; successful completion leads to a Master in Fine Arts and Design accredited by the NVAO. POSITION: the freelance Head of Department role is equivalent to approx. two days per week over the two academic years plus curriculum-development hours (October 2026 to July 2027), at an approximate daily rate of EUR 553.60, supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a department coordinator with administrative support, and institutional infrastructure. WHO CAN APPLY: applicants should have teaching/education experience; be able to develop and coordinate a two-year MA curriculum rooted in their own research and practice; have strong communication and leadership skills; have cross-disciplinary collaborative experience; be committed to an inclusive, supportive learning environment responsive to diverse access needs; be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate; hold an established independent professional practice registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KVK); and, if applying from outside the EU, hold a valid Dutch residence and work permit for the appointment. SELECTION (two rounds): first-round submissions to td@sandberg.nl by 17:00 on Sunday 5 July 2026 (a max. 500-word department proposal, a max. 200-word networks and collaborations statement, and a max. 2-page CV); first-round notification 15 July 2026; interviews with shortlisted candidates 16 September 2026. Questions: td@sandberg.nl.

Open call details

item.title: EIC Accelerator 2026 (European Innovation Council)
item.description:
Organisation: European Innovation Council (European Commission)

Location: EU Member States and Horizon Europe Associated Countries. Single-company applicants; third-country applicants must relocate before the full-application stage.

Award: Grant up to EUR 2.5 million (non-dilutive lump sum) for innovation activities, plus optional equity investment of EUR 1-10 million via the EIC Fund. Options: grant-only, blended finance, or investment-only. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; supports development and scale-up at TRL 5/6 to 8.

Deadline: 08 Jul 2026

The EIC Accelerator funds high-risk, high-impact deep-tech SMEs and start-ups to develop and scale breakthrough innovations. It offers a grant of up to EUR 2.5 million (a non-dilutive lump sum) for innovation activities, and can combine this with an equity investment of EUR 1-10 million through the EIC Fund (blended finance), or provide investment only. The Step 1 short application can be submitted at any time; full (Step 2) applications are evaluated at fixed cut-offs. The next 2026 cut-offs are 8 July, 2 September and 4 November 2026 (all 17:00 Brussels time). Eligible applicants are for-profit SMEs and start-ups (fewer than 250 employees, turnover up to EUR 50M or balance sheet up to EUR 43M) established in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe Associated Country; small mid-caps up to 499 employees are eligible for investment-only support. Apply via the EU Funding and Tenders portal (topic HORIZON-EIC-2026-ACCELERATOR-01).

Open call details

item.title: Treehouse NDSM: Open Call, Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture) Exhibition 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Treehouse NDSM

Location: Treehouse NDSM Pavilion, NDSM, Amsterdam-Noord, Netherlands; particular interest in artists from Amsterdam Noord.

Award: An artist fee is provided to all participating artists. Selected works are exhibited on the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion. No application fee. (Artist fee amount not stated.)

Duration: Exhibition 17 September to 11 October 2026

Deadline: 28 Jun 2026

Treehouse NDSM invites artists to apply for its next exhibition, 'Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture)', running 17 September to 11 October 2026. In the context of current ecological crises, the exhibition takes the constant interaction between culture and nature as its starting point, noting how societies shape the land (from Japanese gardens to post-Soviet geometric parks, Dutch dikes and polders, and Indigenous Australian cultural burning) and how environments shape societies in turn. Artists are invited to reflect on questions such as: how do nature and culture share the same ground? How has your community shaped the land, and how has the land shaped your community? The exhibition is part of Art Park, a series launched at Treehouse in 2023 that reflects on urban nature, particularly in Amsterdam-Noord. The organisers welcome projects that do not offer answers but dig into questions and leave room for uncertainty, across media including performance, painting, making, installation and video. SELECTION: the committee (artist Alina Bielun and artistic coordinator Mulan Go) will select up to ten artworks for the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion, considering both relevance to the theme and the chosen medium, with particular interest in the voices of artists from Amsterdam Noord (temporary or performative works that meaningfully complement the exhibition are also welcome). An artist fee is provided to all participants. Treehouse NDSM is not a traditional gallery but a multidisciplinary incubator offering 100 affordable studios, exhibition spaces and a collaborative artistic community. Submission deadline: 28 June 2026. Apply via the online form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Circolo del Design: Earthrise 26 Residency for Young Designers 2026 (Turin)
item.description:
Organisation: Circolo del Design

Location: Turin, Italy; in-person residency.

Award: Designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included); travel and accommodation covered for both phases of the residency; a public transport pass; up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production; a dedicated 30 sqm workspace within Circolo del Design; plus networking, mentorship from interdisciplinary experts, and technical, logistical and organisational support. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 5 October to 3 December 2026

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Earthrise 26 (Design for a Living Planet), the annual cultural initiative by Circolo del Design, will unfold around clay and earthenware, approached not as functional objects but as narrative, spatial and speculative matter. One of the projects featured in the exhibition will be developed through the Earthrise 26 Residency (5 October to 3 December 2026), a unique opportunity for an under-35 international designer to create and experiment within an interdisciplinary context. Under the mentorship of Designregio Kortrijk and BASE Milano among others, the selected resident will collaborate with local designers, artisans and interdisciplinary experts in Turin to produce an original body of work exhibited as part of the Earthrise 26 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: under 35; a demonstrated international practice through research, collaborations, cultural exchange or mobility within Europe; well-structured project planning with clear goals; thematic relevance aligned with the objectives of Earthrise 2026; and an experimental and/or innovative approach. WHAT IS OFFERED: a designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included), travel and accommodation for both phases, a public transport pass, up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production, a dedicated 30 sqm workspace, and mentorship and technical/logistical/organisational support. Organised by Circolo del Design (circolodeldesign.it; contact programma@circolodeldesign.it). Deadline: 3 July 2026; results announced 20 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: AIR Taipei: International Artist Residency 2027 (Taipei Artist Village & Treasure Hill)
item.description:
Organisation: Taipei Culture Foundation (AIR Taipei, operating Taipei Artist Village and Treasure Hill Artist Village)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan; on-site residency.

Award: Funding varies by track. INTERNATIONAL RESIDENCY (open to international artists): free accommodation and studio space plus administrative and project support; no cash artist grant. PUPPETRY RESIDENCY (Treasure Hill x Puppetry Art Center, open to international artists): NT$20,000 (around USD 620) subsidy plus accommodation and studio. OVERSEAS RESIDENCY VISIT (Taiwanese artists only): NT$100,000 (around USD 3,100) creative grant plus economy-class roundtrip airfare. No application fee.

Duration: Approx. 6 weeks to 3 months depending on category (International Residency roughly 8 to 12 weeks), in 2027

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

AIR Taipei is the international artist-in-residence program run by the Taipei Culture Foundation across the Taipei Artist Village and Treasure Hill Artist Village. The 2027 open call welcomes artists, curators, researchers, performers, writers, cultural practitioners and interdisciplinary creators working in visual art, performance, music, architecture, literature, curatorial practice, cultural studies and interdisciplinary arts. TRACKS AND ELIGIBILITY: international applicants can apply to the International Residency (free accommodation and studio space plus administrative and project support, roughly 8 to 12 weeks, no cash artist grant) and to the Treasure Hill x Puppetry Art Center Project Residency (NT$20,000, around USD 620, subsidy plus accommodation and studio). The Overseas Residency Visit, which carries a NT$100,000 (around USD 3,100) creative grant and economy-class roundtrip airfare, is open to Taiwanese artists only and does not apply to international applicants. Residency length runs approximately 6 weeks to 3 months depending on the category and host institution, taking place in 2027. International applicants must submit a resume and residency plan in English or Chinese. APPLY at https://air-artistvillage.org.tw/ before 11:59:59 (Taipei time, late morning) on Tuesday 30 June 2026. Questions: air@artistvillage.org.

item.title: PLAY26 Creative Gaming Festival: Open Call for Games & Playful Media Works
item.description:
Organisation: PLAY - Creative Gaming Festival

Location: Hamburg, Germany; international submissions welcome (with a Focus Hamburg feature for artists and developers based in Hamburg).

Award: Exhibition and awards opportunity, NOT a paid grant: selected works are showcased to an international festival audience. Awards include the Most Creative Game Award and an Audience Award (all games in the festival exhibition are eligible, voted on by festival visitors). No artist fee is stated.

Duration: Festival 2026 (exhibition and awards show)

Deadline: 28 Jun 2026

PLAY is all about the creative use of digital games, combining this culture with media art, discourse and education. This year's theme, 'Hey, listen! (Music & Emotion)', relates to mixtape culture and the sharing of bonds and feelings with the people you like and love. The open call invites artists and developers to submit games or playful media works (including alt.ctrl, interactive theatre and performances) for one of three strands: the Creative Gaming Awards (works that let players get creative in or with them, including the Most Creative Game Award); the core exhibition 'Hey, listen!' (works fitting this year's Music & Emotion theme); and Focus Hamburg (a Local Artists Feature for artists and developers based in Hamburg). All games shown in the festival exhibition are eligible for the Audience Award, chosen by festival visitors and honoured at the Awards Show. Selected projects are showcased to an international audience. NOTE: this is a festival exhibition and awards open call rather than a paid grant; no artist fee is stated. The Call for Games & Playful Media Works is open until 28 June 2026 (Calls for Talks and Workshops follow). Submit via https://www.playfestival.de/en/play26-submissions-are-open.

item.title: SVĚTOVA 1: As We Grow V (Educational & Research Program) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: SVĚTOVA 1

Location: Prague, Czech Republic; participants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program.

Award: Scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200). Free 14-day educational programme; work exhibited at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks with production and PR support. NO accommodation provided (funding restrictions). No application fee.

Duration: 14 days, 7 to 20 September 2026

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

SVĚTOVA 1 invites early-career artists and cultural organizers to apply for the fifth edition of its educational and research programme As We Grow. This year the course is led by artist and mythopoet András Cséfalvay, Associate Professor at the Academy of Fine Arts in Bratislava. The 14-day project (7 to 20 September 2026) culminates in a group exhibition. WHAT YOU GET: a free 14-day educational experience; a scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200); a rich working environment with on-site interaction with the leading artist, scholars and other artists; active involvement in the As We Grow research and event programming; organizational and professional support; and exhibition of your work at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks, with production and PR support before, during and after the show. SVĚTOVA 1 actively encourages queer and BIPoC people who have migrated to or taken refuge in Prague to apply. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program, have essential spoken English, and commit to the whole 14 days after acceptance. Note: accommodation cannot be offered due to funding restrictions. APPLY via the online Notion form with a portfolio of 3 works, a CV (max 2 pages), and either a 2-3 minute video or a 250-word motivation letter (may include images) outlining how your lived experience and interest align with the course. Deadline: 30 June 2026 at midnight; results announced by 15 July 2026. Questions: tomas@svetova1.cz.

item.title: Civic Science Postdoctoral Fellow: Science Policy Collaborative (George Mason University) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: George Mason University (with UC Riverside), via the Science Policy Collaborative and Civic Science Fellows network

Location: Fairfax, Virginia, USA (hybrid eligible).

Award: Salary approximately USD 80,000 per year plus benefits. No application fee.

Duration: 18-month postdoctoral appointment (two contracts: 12 months + 6 months)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

A grant-funded, full-time Civic Science Postdoctoral Fellowship supporting the Science Policy Collaborative, a national network that strengthens the development, sustainability and evaluation of U.S. science policy programs. The fellow helps shape an emerging field at the heart of a growing community of practice connecting science policy programs across the country. WHAT YOU'LL DO: conduct a national needs assessment of U.S. science policy programs and publish the findings; support the Collaborative's working groups by scoping deliverables and building partnerships to execute them; develop infrastructure for knowledge exchange (tools like logic models and evaluation frameworks); connect programs with national and international experts on the use of research evidence (URE) and evidence-informed policymaking; present at partner meetings and convenings; and receive mentorship from George Mason University, UC Riverside and other leaders of the Collaborative. WHO THEY'RE LOOKING FOR: someone with a doctoral degree, relevant experience, knowledge of science-for-policy programs, and skills in network coordination, needs assessment or program evaluation, and writing for broad audiences (see the posting for exact criteria). Fairfax, VA (hybrid eligible); full-time 18-month postdoctoral appointment issued as two contracts (12 months plus 6 months); salary about USD 80,000/year plus benefits. APPLY with a cover letter, CV, three references and a writing sample written for broad audiences; for full consideration apply by 5 July 2026 (open until filled). Questions to search coordinator Natalie V Lapidot-Croitoru, nlapidot@gmu.edu.

Open call details
